observatory_hook: Authority-weighted evidence matrix | regulator-action timeline | jurisdiction map.
interpretation_note: This remains LIMEN's strongest paper-grade official-action family, but geography and court follow-through still need widening through other families rather than overclaiming completeness here.

observatory_hook: Harm-taxonomy note | comparator-overlap panel | taxonomy-overlay board | related-work table.
interpretation_note: These families stay valuable for methods, comparator baselines, and taxonomy overlays, but they should not be narrated as stand-alone authority for legal status, adjudication, or incident truth.

observatory_hook: Jurisdiction/language map | lead-to-authority upgrade funnel | rare-language methods note.
interpretation_note: This family keeps rare-language and local-context discovery visible while preserving the rule that translation-assisted media or broadcaster surfaces are upgrade routes, not final legal conclusions.

observatory_hook: Lead queue | corroboration backlog | residual-category queue | source-upgrade funnel.
interpretation_note: These families remain governed frontier queues that strengthen novelty, residual-category stress tests, and intake methods without yet supporting seeded evidence or shared empirical claims.

observatory_hook: Research-integrity dashboard facet | publisher-notice upgrade funnel | scholarly-integrity timeline.
interpretation_note: This family remains publication-useful because it separates direct notice text, archived notice text, mediated metadata, and blocked or residual rows instead of flattening scholarly-integrity posture.

observatory_hook: Regulator-to-court escalation matrix | Route-B blocker panel | procedural-posture timeline | public-sector proof-ceiling panel | security hardening panel.
interpretation_note: These remain the decisive thin families for stronger hostile-reviewer resilience: sf08 still gates open court text, sf09 now carries a checked same-lineage official-route no-yield ceiling while buyer-side/public-impact proof remains missing, and sf11 now also has synchronized lane-local route-entry surfaces but still gates one venue-grade incident-specific technical case attachment even after the published AIES disclosure-context check.

observatory_hook: Security/agentic crosswalk | remediation-visibility overlay | interoperability board | technique-coverage heatmap.
interpretation_note: These families remain valuable for mechanism, remediation, and interoperability context, but they should not be treated as final authority on realized incident truth or broad prevalence.
